cast.voice_direction: fast, urgent, brilliant, warm, slightly chaotic

lines.text: Temporal field is stable enough for testing. Keep your speed under control, watch the circuits, and do not tear history in half.
The courthouse clock is our anchor. If the square changes, check the plaque, the diner sign, and the archive wall.

cast.role: player ally, field mechanic, mission navigator
cast.voice_direction: grounded, smart, brave, dry humor under pressure

lines.text: I marked the safe route around the square. Drive clean, calibrate the sensor, and come back in one piece.
If you exit the car, stay close to the objective markers. This town gets weird fast.
No speeding through courthouse square. I do not care what year you say you are from.
Storm is rolling in. Keep civilians away from the cable route and move.

cast.role: historian, archive witness, timeline continuity expert
cast.voice_direction: clear, observant, calm under impossible evidence

lines.text: The archive records changed again. The founder portrait, school dedication, and diner menu no longer agree.
Photograph everything before you jump. Memory is unreliable after a paradox wave.

cast.voice_direction: cocky, taunting, dangerous but not cartoonish

lines.text: Nice wheels. Shame you drive like you are afraid of the future.
This square belongs to whoever controls the clock. Try to keep up.

cast.role: diner owner and era-specific rumor hub
cast.voice_direction: warm, sharp, observant, classic small-town gossip energy

lines.text: You look like trouble with a steering wheel. Sit down, order coffee, and tell me which year is missing.
The clocktower crowd has been jumpy all morning. Something about lightning and a car that hums.
That machine of yours spooked every horse from here to the rail yard.
If you need the clock repaired, start with the blacksmith and keep clear of the noon train.
Welcome to 2045. The city remembers every timeline you broke to get here.
Your vehicle is analog, unstable, and somehow beautiful. Do not let the grid scan it.
